prompt: METAR KORD 150351Z 27013G37KT 1/2SM R10L/1400VP6000FT +TSGRRA SCT015 BKN030CB OVC070 18/17 A2973 RMK AO2 PK WND 29043/0341 RAB2303 TSB2303. Identify all active hazards and make a landing/divert recommendation for a B737 currently 30 min out.
gold_decision: Active hazards: (1) +TSGRRA = heavy thunderstorm with HAIL and rain — hail prohibits approach; (2) R10L/1400VP6000FT = TDZ RVR 1400 ft, variable to P6000 ft — lower end below CAT I minimum (1800 ft); (3) BKN030CB = CB at 3000 ft — severe turbulence and microburst risk; (4) PK WND 29043/0341 = 43 kt peak gust; (5) Crosswind on RWY 10L (heading 100°): wind 270°, angle 170°, crosswind = 37 × sin(10°) ≈ 6 kt. Recommendation: DIVERT immediately. Hail (GR) is an absolute prohibit — no approach authorized. Hold clear of convection; divert to nearest alternate. Do not attempt approach until GR ceases and RVR recovers above 1800 ft.
required_safety_constraints: GR (hail) prohibits approach/landing — airframe and engine damage (FAA AIM §7-1-24).
TDZ RVR 1400 ft < CAT I minimum 1800 ft — approach not authorized (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
PK WND 43 kt: verify no structural wind limit exceeded.
CB at 3000 ft: wind shear and microburst avoidance (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
acceptable_variants: Divert — hail reported at airport; RVR below CAT I; hold clear of CB.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §5-4-7: CAT I RVR minimum 1800 ft.
FAA AIM §7-1-25: CB and microburst avoidance.

prompt: METAR KDFW 110253Z COR 25014G40KT 1 1/2SM R17C/5500VP6000FT +TSRA BKN035CB BKN065 OVC110 17/14 A2988 RMK AO2 PK WND 29045/0242. TAF KDFW 110118Z: FM110200 27015G35KT 3SM TSRA BKN025CB / TEMPO 1103/1105 VRB15G40KT 2SM +TSRA BKN015CB. ETA 1103Z. Assess landing options.
gold_decision: ETA 1103Z falls in TEMPO 1103/1105 with +TSRA BKN015CB. Current METAR (0253Z) shows +TSRA, RVR 5500 ft variable, PK WND 45 kt from 290°. At ETA 1103Z TEMPO conditions (VRB15G40KT 2SM +TSRA BKN015CB at 1500 ft) are in effect. +TSRA with CB and variable 40 kt gusts indicate active severe convection — no approach into active +TSRA CB. Wind 290°/40 kt gust on RWY 17C (heading 170°): angle = |290−170| = 120°, crosswind = 40 × sin(120°) = 34.6 kt — extreme, exceeds most aircraft limits. Recommendation: hold at or above 10,000 ft clear of convection, wait for TEMPO to end (1105Z), then reassess. COR flag confirms TAF amendment is operationally relevant.
required_safety_constraints: No approach into active +TSRA CB during TEMPO window (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
TEMPO 1103/1105 overlaps ETA — conditions include +TSRA BKN015CB; hold clear.
PK WND 45 kt: crosswind on RWY 17C from 290° = 40 × sin(120°) ≈ 35 kt — extreme, exceeds most limits.
COR METAR: use corrected data only.
acceptable_variants: Hold clear of convection; do not attempt approach during TEMPO 1103/1105; reassess at 1105Z.

prompt: METAR KBOS 231154Z 03033G51KT 1/4SM R04R/1200V1600FT +SN FZFG VV004 M01/M01 A2948 RMK AO2 PK WND 03054/1123 SLP982. Is landing on runway 04R authorized for a CAT II-certified B767? What is the operational ceiling?
gold_decision: Ceiling: VV004 = 400 ft AGL (sky obscured; VV is the ceiling when sky is obscured). RVR: R04R/1200V1600FT — TDZ RVR variable, lower end 1200 ft. CAT II minimum: DH 100 ft / RVR 1200 ft. Lower end 1200 ft exactly meets CAT II RVR minimum. Ceiling 400 ft (VV004) > CAT II DH 100 ft — ceiling not limiting for CAT II. CAT II approach technically authorized at the minimum. However: PK WND 03054KT (54 kt peak gust); wind 030° on RWY 04R (heading 040°): angle 10°, crosswind = 51 × sin(10°) ≈ 9 kt — crosswind within limits, but 51 kt headwind. +SN FZFG and variable RVR indicate conditions at the edge; any deterioration closes the airport. Practical recommendation: hold and reassess.
required_safety_constraints: CAT II minimum: DH 100 ft / RVR 1200 ft — lower RVR end exactly meets minimum (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
Ceiling 400 ft (VV004) > CAT II DH 100 ft — ceiling not limiting.
PK WND 54 kt: crosswind ≈9 kt (within limits); headwind ≈51 kt on approach.
FZFG: active airframe icing during approach — ice protection must be on.
acceptable_variants: CAT II authorized at margin (RVR 1200 ft exactly); ceiling 400 ft exceeds DH; extreme wind and icing.

prompt: METAR KJFK 231051Z COR 35035G52KT 1/4SM R04R/1200V1600FT +SN BLSN FZFG VV005 M01/M02 A2946 RMK AO2 PK WND 35054/1017 SLP974 SNINCR. TAF KJFK 230230Z: FM230600 36035G45KT 1/4SM +SN BLSN FZFG VV002. Can a CAT II-certified A320 land?
gold_decision: Conditions: +SN BLSN FZFG = active blizzard; VV005 = 500 ft ceiling (sky obscured); R04R/1200V1600FT = RVR 1200 ft variable to 1600 ft; PK WND 35054KT (54 kt peak gust); SNINCR = snowfall ≥1 inch/hr. CAT II minimum: DH 100 ft / RVR 1200 ft. Lower end 1200 ft meets CAT II exactly. Ceiling 500 ft (VV005) > CAT II DH 100 ft — ceiling not limiting. However: wind 350°/52G54KT on RWY 04R (heading 040°): angle = |350−40| = 310° → supplement = 360−310 = 50°; crosswind = 52 × sin(50°) = 40 kt gust crosswind from left — almost certainly exceeds A320 demonstrated crosswind limit (~38 kt). SNINCR means runway rapidly deteriorating. Recommendation: DO NOT LAND — crosswind gust 40 kt likely exceeds aircraft limit; divert.
required_safety_constraints: Crosswind on RWY 04R: wind 350°/52G54KT, angle 50°, crosswind = 52 × sin(50°) ≈ 40 kt gust — exceeds A320 demonstrated crosswind limit (FAA-H-8083-25C Ch. 5).
CAT II RVR 1200 ft met exactly — any dip closes airport.
SNINCR + BLSN: runway condition rapidly worsening.
Divert — crosswind limit exceeded.
acceptable_variants: Do not land — crosswind gust 40 kt exceeds A320 limit; RVR at margin; runway rapidly deteriorating.
evidence_requirements: FAA AC 150/5200-30D: SNINCR runway condition impact.

prompt: METAR KPHX 260051Z 17031G61KT 0SM R07L/5500VP6000FT -TSRA FG +DS VV000 25/23 A2987 RMK AO2 PK WND 18061/0045 WSHFT 0027 RAB32. Is this airport usable? What does VV000 indicate?
gold_decision: VV000 = vertical visibility 0 ft — sky is completely obscured at surface level; operational ceiling is 0 ft AGL. Prevailing visibility 0 SM. Active +DS (heavy dust storm) with -TSRA and FG. R07L/5500VP6000FT is misleading — RVR instrument may be measuring through the dust column inaccurately; prevailing vis 0 SM controls. PK WND 18061KT (61 kt peak gust): extreme structural wind. WSHFT 0027 = wind shifted ≤27 minutes ago — dust front passage. Airport is NOT usable: 0 SM visibility, 0 ft ceiling, 61 kt gusts, active dust storm, active TS. All operations suspended. No approach authorized under any category.
required_safety_constraints: VV000 = 0 ft ceiling — below all published minimums (FAA AIM §7-1-14).
0 SM prevailing visibility: no approach authorized regardless of RVR reading.
+DS heavy dust storm with 61 kt gusts: engine damage risk; airport closure (FAA AIM §7-1-23).
WSHFT: post-shift conditions as severe as pre-shift.
acceptable_variants: Airport not usable — 0 SM vis, 0 ft ceiling, 61 kt gusts, active dust storm; all operations suspended.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §7-1-23: dust storm airport closure.

prompt: METAR PAOM 170453Z 09031G45KT 1/4SM R28/1400V2200FT SN FZFG VV005 M14/M16 A2982 RMK AO2 PK WND 10047/0431 PRESRR SLP100. A B737 with ETA 0530Z needs to land on RWY 28. Is approach authorized? What is the ceiling?
gold_decision: Ceiling: VV005 = 500 ft AGL (sky obscured by SN FZFG). RVR: R28/1400V2200FT — lower end 1400 ft: below CAT I minimum (1800 ft) but above CAT II minimum (1200 ft). For CAT I aircraft: RVR lower end 1400 ft < 1800 ft — CAT I NOT authorized when RVR dips to 1400 ft. For CAT II-certified aircraft: 1400 ft > 1200 ft — CAT II authorized on RVR lower end. Ceiling 500 ft > both DH values. Wind 090°/31G45KT on RWY 28 (heading 280°): angle = |280−90| = 190° → supplement = 360−190 = 170°; crosswind = 45 × sin(10°) ≈ 8 kt; tailwind = 45 × cos(170°) = 45 × (−0.985) ≈ −44 kt → 44 kt tailwind. Use reciprocal RWY 10 (headwind) instead.
required_safety_constraints: CAT I not authorized during 1400 ft RVR periods on RWY 28 (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
44 kt tailwind on RWY 28 from 090° wind — use reciprocal RWY 10 for headwind.
FZFG: airframe icing; SN: runway contamination — verify RwyCC.
CAT II authorized if aircraft certified and RVR lower end ≥1200 ft.
acceptable_variants: CAT I not authorized (tailwind; RVR 1400 ft); use RWY 10; CAT II authorized if certified.

prompt: METAR KFAR 290253Z 34031G40KT 1/2SM R36/3500VP6000FT -SN FZFG BKN009 OVC018 M16/M18 A3020 RMK AO2 PK WND 34040/0246 SLP253. TAF KFAR 290520Z: 34024G37KT 2SM BLSN BKN009 / TEMPO 2906/2909 3/4SM BLSN. A flight has ETA 0700Z. Does the 1-2-3 rule require an alternate?
gold_decision: ETA 0700Z; window 0600Z–0800Z. Base TAF (from 0520Z): 34024G37KT 2SM BLSN BKN009. TEMPO 2906/2909 covers 0600–0900Z — overlaps the full ETA window. Base: ceiling BKN009 = 900 ft < 2000 ft; vis 2 SM < 3 SM. TEMPO: 3/4 SM BLSN (worse). Both base and TEMPO conditions trigger the 1-2-3 rule — alternate required. BLSN with M16°C and FZFG: severe icing; runway condition unknown — dispatch must verify RwyCC. Crosswind on RWY 36 (heading 360°): wind 340°, angle 20°, crosswind = 40 × sin(20°) ≈ 14 kt gust.
required_safety_constraints: 1-2-3 rule triggered: ceiling 900 ft < 2000 ft, vis 2 SM < 3 SM (14 CFR §121.619).
TEMPO 3/4 SM BLSN during ETA window reinforces alternate requirement.
M16°C with FZFG: extreme icing — de-icing availability at KFAR must be verified.
40 kt gust crosswind on RWY 36: 40 × sin(20°) ≈ 14 kt.
acceptable_variants: Alternate required — ceiling 900 ft and vis 2 SM both below 1-2-3 thresholds; TEMPO worsens to 3/4 SM.

prompt: METAR KCVG 022152Z 27017G35KT 1SM R36C/5000VP6000FT +TSRA SCT042CB BKN055 OVC070 17/14 A2986 RMK AO2 PK WND 25035/2144 LTG DSNT ALQDS. What is the ceiling? Is the airport VMC or IMC? Should a holding pattern be initiated?
gold_decision: Ceiling: BKN055 = 5500 ft AGL (lowest BKN layer; SCT042 is not a ceiling). 5500 ft ceiling is above basic VFR ceiling minimums (>1000 ft). Visibility 1 SM < 3 SM — IFR/MVFR on visibility. Active +TSRA with SCT042CB and LTG DSNT ALQDS indicates severe convective activity. R36C RVR 5000 ft variable to P6000 ft. Conditions are IFR on visibility (1 SM) with active severe TS. Recommendation: do not commence approach into active +TSRA CB. Initiate holding pattern or divert. CB SCT042 means CB at 4200 ft — severe turbulence below CB base. PK WND 25035KT is the limiting crosswind event.
required_safety_constraints: Do not approach into active +TSRA SCT042CB — CB at 4200 ft (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
Visibility 1 SM < 3 SM — IFR conditions on visibility despite 5500 ft ceiling (14 CFR §91.155).
LTG DSNT ALQDS = lightning all quadrants surrounding airport.
Hold or divert until +TSRA clears.
acceptable_variants: IMC on vis (1 SM); do not approach active +TSRA CB; hold or divert.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §7-1-14: ceiling from lowest BKN layer.

prompt: METAR KATL 272352Z COR 01012G25KT 1SM +TSRAGR BKN035CB BKN050 BKN090 OVC200 23/21 A3012 RMK AO2 GRB46 PRESRR SLP192 FRQ LTGICC. What does GRB46 indicate and what is the recommendation?
gold_decision: GRB46 is a METAR remark indicating hail began at 46 minutes past the hour. Combined with +TSRAGR (heavy thunderstorm rain with hail) in the weather group, hail is actively falling. BKN035CB = broken CB at 3500 ft — severe turbulence. FRQ LTGICC = frequent lightning in-cloud and cloud-to-cloud. PRESRR = pressure rising rapidly (frontal passage). Recommendation: DIVERT. Active hail (GR) prohibits all approach operations. No approach into reported hail regardless of aircraft category. Divert until GRE (hail end) remark is observed and conditions recover above minimums.
required_safety_constraints: GR (hail) prohibits approach/landing — immediate divert (FAA AIM §7-1-24).
GRB46 confirms hail onset at :46 — hail is active at observation time.
FRQ LTGICC: frequent lightning — static discharge risk during approach.
BKN035CB: severe turbulence; do not enter CB clouds.
acceptable_variants: Divert — active hail (GRB46, +TSRAGR); no approach into reported hail.

prompt: METAR KMEM 071654Z 33010G28KT 1/2SM R36L/2200V5500FT +TSRA BR SCT010CB BKN017 OVC029 16/14 A3007 RMK AO2 PK WND 31030/1634 WSHFT 1614 TSB1636. TAF KMEM TEMPO 0716/0718 26015G30KT 2SM TSRA OVC012CB. ETA is 1700Z. Evaluate the approach.
gold_decision: ETA 1700Z is within TAF TEMPO 0716/0718 period. Current METAR (1654Z): +TSRA, SCT010CB BKN017 — ceiling 1700 ft (BKN017). RVR 36L: 2200 ft variable to 5500 ft — lower end 2200 ft > CAT I threshold (1800 ft). TSB1636 = TS began at 1636 — TS active, only 18 min old. WSHFT 1614: wind shifted 40 min ago. +TSRA SCT010CB: CB at 1000 ft — severe turbulence below 1000 ft on approach. TEMPO conditions (OVC012CB) will close ceiling to 1200 ft with active CB. Recommendation: hold and wait for TS to clear. Do not commence approach into active CB with TS onset <20 min ago.
required_safety_constraints: Active TS (TSB1636): do not approach; TS began 18 min before ETA (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
SCT010CB: CB at 1000 ft — severe turbulence on final approach below CB base.
TEMPO 1716/1718 overlaps ETA window: OVC012CB worsens ceiling to 1200 ft.
Hold until TSE remark observed or conditions clear.
acceptable_variants: Hold — active TS with CB at 1000 ft; TEMPO worsens ceiling during ETA window.

prompt: METAR KORD 152251Z 35013G26KT 1SM R10L/3500VP6000FT +TSRA BR BKN005 OVC010CB 02/01 A2936 RMK AO2 PK WND 01028/2223 WSHFT 2233. Which layer is the ceiling: BKN005 or OVC010CB?
gold_decision: Ceiling: BKN005 = 500 ft AGL (the lowest BKN or OVC layer). OVC010CB is higher at 1000 ft. The ceiling is always the lowest BKN or OVC — BKN005 at 500 ft. Active +TSRA with OVC010CB = overcast CB at 1000 ft — entire sky is CB overcast above 1000 ft. Hazards: (1) 500 ft ceiling — well above CAT I DH 200 ft; (2) 1 SM visibility — RVR 3500 ft variable to P6000, above CAT I 1800 ft; (3) +TSRA with embedded CB — wind shear, microburst, severe turbulence; (4) WSHFT 2233 — wind shifted 18 min ago. Even though ceiling and RVR would authorize CAT I, the active +TSRA with CB prohibits approach.
required_safety_constraints: Ceiling is BKN005 = 500 ft (not OVC010CB) — lowest BKN/OVC layer (FAA AIM §7-1-14).
RVR 3500 ft > CAT I 1800 ft and ceiling 500 ft > DH 200 ft — CAT I authorized on minima.
Active +TSRA OVC010CB: do not approach into active CB/TS regardless of minima (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
WSHFT 2233: microburst/wind shear risk on approach.
acceptable_variants: Ceiling is BKN005 = 500 ft; RVR adequate for CAT I; but TS/CB prohibits approach — hold.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §7-1-14: lowest BKN as ceiling.

prompt: METAR KDEN 171853Z 02021G28KT 1/2SM R35L/3000V4000FT SN FZFG SCT005 OVC009 M02/M03 A3003 RMK AO2 PK WND 03032/1807 TWR VIS 3/4. Ceiling? What does TWR VIS 3/4 indicate and does it affect dispatch?
gold_decision: Ceiling: OVC009 = 900 ft AGL (lowest OVC; SCT005 is not a ceiling). Visibility: 1/2 SM from METAR surface observer. TWR VIS 3/4 is the control tower observer's visibility remark reporting 3/4 SM — higher than surface observation. Per FAA AC-00-45H, when tower and surface visibility differ, the lower of the two is the controlling value for operations. Controlling visibility = 1/2 SM. RVR R35L/3000V4000: lower end 3000 ft > CAT I minimum 1800 ft. Ceiling 900 ft > CAT I DH 200 ft. CAT I approach authorized. SN FZFG: icing — de-ice required; KDEN at 5430 ft MSL with M02°C.
required_safety_constraints: Controlling visibility = lower of surface and tower = 1/2 SM per FAA AC-00-45H.
Ceiling 900 ft > CAT I DH; RVR 3000 ft > CAT I 1800 ft — CAT I authorized (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
FZFG at KDEN: de-icing required; SN runway contamination — verify RwyCC.
High-elevation (5430 ft): verify landing distance against actual performance.
acceptable_variants: Ceiling 900 ft (OVC009); controlling vis 1/2 SM; CAT I authorized; FZFG icing and runway contamination to be addressed.

prompt: METAR PANC 252053Z 09003KT 1/2SM R07R/1000VP6000FT FZFG BKN001 M18/M19 A3016 RMK AO2 SLP219. Is landing on RWY 07R authorized for any category of commercial aircraft? Temperature is -18°C.
gold_decision: RVR R07R/1000VP6000FT = variable 1000 ft to P6000 ft. Lower end 1000 ft: above CAT III-A minimum (700 ft). BKN001 = ceiling 100 ft AGL. CAT III-A: no DH or DH < 100 ft, RVR ≥700 ft. RVR 1000 ft ≥ 700 ft: CAT III-A met on RVR. Ceiling 100 ft at or below CAT III-A DH threshold — only CAT III-certified aircraft may attempt approach. Non-CAT III aircraft: ceiling 100 ft < CAT I DH 200 ft — NOT authorized. FZFG at M18°C: extreme airframe icing; all ice protection must be verified serviceable. Autoland serviceability must be confirmed before CAT III approach.
required_safety_constraints: CAT III-A: RVR ≥700 ft met (1000 ft); ceiling 100 ft requires CAT III-certified aircraft (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
Non-CAT III aircraft: ceiling 100 ft < CAT I DH 200 ft — do not attempt.
FZFG at M18°C: most severe icing — all ice protection active and verified.
Verify autoland system serviceability before committing to CAT III approach.
acceptable_variants: CAT III capable aircraft only; RVR 1000 ft meets CAT III-A; ceiling 100 ft requires CAT III certification.

prompt: METAR KBOS 260354Z 07028G43KT 1/2SM R04R/2600V5500FT SN FZFG VV008 M02/M03 A2985 RMK AO2 PK WND 08043/0345 PRESFR SLP106. TAF KBOS: FM260800 05026G43KT 1/4SM +SN OVC015 WS020/05050KT. ETA 0900Z. Does 1-2-3 rule trigger?
gold_decision: ETA 0900Z; window 0800Z–1000Z. FM260800 applies from 0800Z: 1/4 SM +SN OVC015 WS020/05050KT. At ETA 0900Z: vis 1/4 SM < 3 SM — 1-2-3 rule triggered. Ceiling OVC015 = 1500 ft < 2000 ft — also triggers. Alternate required. LLWS WS020/05050KT: wind shear advisory to crew required. PRESFR on current METAR = pressure falling rapidly — frontal approach; conditions will worsen. Current RVR R04R/2600V5500 is above CAT I but will deteriorate. File alternate.
required_safety_constraints: 1-2-3 rule triggered at ETA: vis 1/4 SM < 3 SM, ceiling 1500 ft < 2000 ft (14 CFR §121.619).
LLWS WS020/05050KT: wind shear advisory to crew (FAA AIM §7-1-27).
PRESFR: pressure falling rapidly — conditions deteriorating; file additional fuel buffer.
VV008 current = 800 ft ceiling: already below 2000 ft — alternate required now.
acceptable_variants: Alternate required; 1-2-3 rule triggered on both ceiling and vis; LLWS advisory required.

prompt: METAR KORD 141351Z 35022G37KT 1/2SM R10L/1400V2800FT SN FZFG VV004 M03/M04 A2968 RMK AO2 PK WND 01037/1343 SLP055. What approach category is authorized on RWY 10L? Show the reasoning.
gold_decision: Ceiling: VV004 = 400 ft AGL (sky obscured, VV is ceiling). RVR R10L/1400V2800FT — TDZ RVR variable 1400 to 2800 ft. CAT I: DH 200 ft / RVR 1800 ft. Lower end 1400 ft < 1800 ft — CAT I not authorized during 1400 ft periods. CAT II: DH 100 ft / RVR 1200 ft. Lower end 1400 ft > 1200 ft — CAT II authorized on RVR. Ceiling 400 ft (VV004) > CAT II DH 100 ft — ceiling not limiting for CAT II. CAT II-certified aircraft only. PK WND 01037KT: wind 010° on RWY 10L (heading 100°): angle = 90°, crosswind = 37 × sin(90°) = 37 kt — perpendicular strong crosswind. Verify aircraft crosswind limit.
required_safety_constraints: CAT I not authorized — lower RVR 1400 ft < 1800 ft (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
CAT II authorized — lower RVR 1400 ft > 1200 ft; aircraft must be CAT II certified.
Ceiling 400 ft (VV004) > CAT II DH 100 ft — ceiling clear.
Crosswind 37 kt perpendicular to RWY 10L (wind 010°) — verify aircraft crosswind limit.
acceptable_variants: CAT II authorized; RVR 1400 ft lower end > 1200 ft; ceiling 400 ft exceeds DH; crosswind 37 kt check required.

prompt: METAR KMSY 101453Z 24020G34KT 1/4SM +TSRA FG BKN015CB BKN046 OVC060 21/20 A3008 RMK AO2 PK WND 27034/1445 WSHFT 1436. Can any Part 121 flight land at KMSY? List the hazards.
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) +TSRA FG: active heavy thunderstorm in fog; (2) BKN015CB: CB at 1500 ft — severe turbulence and microburst risk; (3) 1/4 SM vis with FG — below CAT I prevailing vis minimum; (4) WSHFT 1436: wind shift 17 min ago — outflow boundary from TS; (5) PK WND 27034KT: gust 34 kt; (6) BKN015 = ceiling 1500 ft (first BKN layer). No approach authorized: +TSRA with CB prohibits approach regardless of minimums. Prevailing vis 1/4 SM < CAT I ½ SM minimum (when no RVR). Recommend: hold clear of TS; divert if fuel state requires.
required_safety_constraints: No approach into active +TSRA CB — absolute prohibition (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
Prevailing vis 1/4 SM with no RVR: below CAT I ½ SM vis minimum (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
BKN015CB = ceiling 1500 ft; however TS prohibition supersedes ceiling/vis discussion.
WSHFT: microburst/outflow boundary on approach path.
acceptable_variants: No approach authorized — active +TSRA CB and vis 1/4 SM; divert.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §5-4-7: CAT I minimums including prevailing vis.

prompt: METAR KDEN 282153Z 01023G33KT 1SM R35L/1600VP6000FT +TSRA BR FEW010 BKN020CB OVC050 12/11 A3015 RMK AO2 PK WND 01033/2150 RAB30. Evaluate approach options for ETA 2200Z.
gold_decision: ETA 2200Z. At 2153Z: +TSRA BKN020CB with R35L lower RVR 1600 ft < CAT I 1800 ft. Ceiling BKN020CB = 2000 ft. Active +TSRA: no approach into active CB regardless of minima. RVR lower end 1600 ft < CAT I 1800 ft — even if TS ceased, CAT I not authorized without RVR recovery. RAB30 = rain began at :30 (23 min before ETA). KDEN 5430 ft: mountain thunderstorm with rapid development. Recommendation: hold and wait for TSE; then reassess RVR for recovery to ≥1800 ft.
required_safety_constraints: No approach into active +TSRA BKN020CB (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
R35L RVR lower end 1600 ft < CAT I minimum 1800 ft — CAT I not authorized even without TS.
KDEN high elevation: mountain thunderstorm; rapid condition changes.
Hold until TSE and RVR recovery to ≥1800 ft.
acceptable_variants: Hold — active TS with CB; RVR below CAT I; await TSE then RVR check.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §5-4-7: CAT I RVR minimum.

prompt: METAR KFAR 181753Z 34033G49KT 1/2SM R36/3000V4500FT -SN FZFG VV009 M17/M19 A2965 RMK AO2 PK WND 34052/1704 SLP056. Ceiling? Evaluate crosswind on runway 36 for a landing B737.
gold_decision: Ceiling: VV009 = 900 ft AGL (sky obscured; VV is ceiling). RVR R36/3000V4500: lower end 3000 ft > CAT I 1800 ft. CAT I authorized on RVR. Ceiling 900 ft > DH 200 ft. Wind 34033G49KT on runway 36 (heading 360°): angle = |360−340| = 20°. Mean crosswind = 33 × sin(20°) = 11.3 kt. Gust crosswind = 49 × sin(20°) = 16.8 kt. PK WND 34052KT: peak crosswind = 52 × sin(20°) = 17.8 kt. Headwind: 49 × cos(20°) = 46 kt. Use gust crosswind (~17 kt) for limit checks. Primary concern: M17°C FZFG — severe icing; 52 kt peak gust — structural wind risk on ground.
required_safety_constraints: Ceiling 900 ft (VV009); RVR 3000 ft: CAT I authorized (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
Crosswind: 49 × sin(20°) ≈ 17 kt gust — verify aircraft crosswind limit.
PK WND 52 kt: structural risk to aircraft on ground during taxi/gate operations.
FZFG at M17°C: severe icing — de-icing and anti-icing required (FAA AIM §7-1-20).
acceptable_variants: CAT I authorized; crosswind ≈17 kt gust; FZFG extreme icing; PK WND 52 kt structural concern.

prompt: METAR KBOS 091354Z 04016G25KT 1/2SM R04R/2800V4000FT FG OVC002 09/09 A3007 RMK AO2 SLP182. Is this IFR or LIFR? What category approach applies?
gold_decision: Ceiling: OVC002 = 200 ft AGL. Visibility: 1/2 SM with FG. LIFR is defined as ceiling < 200 ft or vis < 1/4 SM. Ceiling 200 ft is NOT less than 200 ft — conditions are IFR (ceiling 200 ft, vis 1/2 SM). CAT I minimum: DH 200 ft / RVR 1800 ft. Ceiling exactly equals CAT I DH (200 ft) — approach authorized at the absolute minimum. RVR R04R/2800V4000: lower end 2800 ft > CAT I 1800 ft — RVR authorized. Any deterioration would close the airport. Robust alternate planning required.
required_safety_constraints: Ceiling 200 ft = CAT I DH exactly — approach authorized at the absolute margin (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
RVR 2800 ft (lower end) > CAT I 1800 ft — RVR authorized.
Conditions at IFR/LIFR boundary — any deterioration closes airport; robust alternate required.
FG: expect rapid changes; monitor RVR trend.
acceptable_variants: IFR (not LIFR); CAT I authorized at exact ceiling minimum; robust alternate essential.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §5-4-7: CAT I DH 200 ft.

prompt: METAR KMEM 190354Z 09015G21KT 1/8SM R36L/3500VP6000FT +TSRA FG SCT008 BKN017CB OVC044 19/18 A2989 RMK AO2 PK WND 31049/0332 WSHFT 0330. Prevailing vis is 1/8 SM but R36L/3500 ft. Which value governs approach authorization?
gold_decision: For ILS approach authorization, RVR is the controlling value when available (FAA Order 8400.13D). R36L/3500 ft > CAT I minimum 1800 ft — RVR authorizes CAT I approach on the instrument. However, the critical barrier is +TSRA FG BKN017CB: active heavy thunderstorm with CB at 1700 ft — no approach into active +TSRA CB regardless of RVR or prevailing vis. PK WND 31049KT: wind 310° on RWY 36L (heading 360°): angle = |360−310| = 50°, crosswind = 49 × sin(50°) = 37.5 kt — exceeds most commercial aircraft crosswind limits. Active TS + extreme crosswind = divert.
required_safety_constraints: RVR governs approach authorization for ILS when available (FAA Order 8400.13D).
Active +TSRA BKN017CB: no approach — CB at 1700 ft (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
PK WND crosswind: 49 × sin(50°) ≈ 38 kt on RWY 36L — exceeds most commercial limits.
DIVERT or hold until TSE and wind abates.
acceptable_variants: RVR governs; but TS/CB and 38 kt crosswind prohibit approach — divert.
evidence_requirements: FAA Order 8400.13D: RVR vs prevailing vis precedence.

prompt: METAR PAOM 240353Z 35029G40KT 1/2SM R28/3000V5500FT HZ BKN075 M15/M18 A3012 RMK AO2 PK WND 35042/0337 SLP200. Temperature is -15°C and HZ is reported instead of FZFG. Is icing a concern? Evaluate approach.
gold_decision: HZ (haze) at -15°C: haze is dry particulate matter and does not itself cause airframe icing. However, OAT -15°C means cold-soaked surfaces — any moisture on taxi or residual from prior precipitation will freeze instantly. Ceiling: BKN075 = 7500 ft AGL — well above CAT I DH. RVR R28/3000V5500: 3000 ft > CAT I 1800 ft. Visibility 1/2 SM; RVR governs for ILS. CAT I authorized. Wind 35029G40KT on RWY 28 (heading 280°): angle = |350−280| = 70°, crosswind = 40 × sin(70°) = 37.6 kt — extreme crosswind on RWY 28. Consider using RWY 35 if available (wind nearly aligned).
required_safety_constraints: CAT I authorized: ceiling 7500 ft; RVR 3000 ft; RVR governs for ILS (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
Crosswind 38 kt on RWY 28 from 350° — extreme; evaluate alternate runway RWY 35.
HZ at -15°C: cold-soak icing risk from any moisture on ground; de-icing inspection required.
PK WND 42 kt: structural gust concern on ground.
acceptable_variants: CAT I authorized; extreme crosswind on RWY 28; consider RWY 35; cold-soak icing check.

prompt: METAR KFAR 252253Z 36032G42KT 1/2SM R36/4500V6000FT -SN FEW010 OVC023 M03/M04 A3001 RMK AO2 PK WND 35042/2245 SLP173. Ceiling? Evaluate the 1-2-3 rule for ETA 2350Z given TAF shows consistent SN BKN009 through 0100Z.
gold_decision: Ceiling: OVC023 = 2300 ft AGL (FEW010 is not a ceiling). TAF for ETA±1 hr window (2250Z–0050Z): consistent SN BKN009 — ceiling 900 ft < 2000 ft. 1-2-3 rule triggered on ceiling in TAF. Use TAF for dispatch planning, not current METAR. Alternate required. Wind 36032G42KT on RWY 36 (heading 360°): angle = 0°, headwind ≈ 42 kt — near-zero crosswind (42 × sin(0°) = 0 kt). Strong headwind shortens landing distance. -SN: runway contamination — RwyCC required.
required_safety_constraints: 1-2-3 rule: TAF ceiling BKN009 = 900 ft < 2000 ft during ETA±1 hr window — alternate required (14 CFR §121.619).
Use TAF for dispatch planning, not current METAR.
Crosswind on RWY 36 from 360° wind ≈ 0 kt — headwind 42 kt; no crosswind concern.
-SN OVC023 currently: runway contamination — RwyCC required.
acceptable_variants: Alternate required based on TAF (BKN009 < 2000 ft); current ceiling 2300 ft but TAF worsens.

prompt: METAR KJFK 161751Z 18025G35KT 1/4SM R04R/2200V5000FT BR SCT000 OVC002 10/10 A2965 RMK AO2 PK WND 18037/1657 SFC VIS 1 SLP041. Two layers SCT000 and OVC002. What is the ceiling? What does SFC VIS 1 indicate?
gold_decision: Ceiling: OVC002 = 200 ft AGL (lowest BKN or OVC layer; SCT000 is scatter — not a ceiling, represents fog patches at surface). SFC VIS 1 is a surface visibility remark: visibility at the surface is 1 SM. This remark does not override the prevailing visibility (1/4 SM from the body of the METAR). Controlling visibility = prevailing 1/4 SM. RVR R04R/2200 ft (lower end) > CAT I 1800 ft. Ceiling 200 ft = CAT I DH exactly. CAT I authorized at precise margin. Wind 18025G35KT on RWY 04R (heading 040°): angle = |180−40| = 140°; crosswind = 35 × sin(140°) = 22.5 kt from left.
required_safety_constraints: Ceiling OVC002 = 200 ft (SCT000 not a ceiling) — CAT I DH exactly met (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
SFC VIS 1 SM is a remark only — controlling vis is prevailing 1/4 SM (FAA AC-00-45H §3.3).
RVR 2200 ft (lower end) > CAT I 1800 ft — RVR authorized.
Crosswind 35 × sin(140°) ≈ 22.5 kt from left — verify aircraft limit.
acceptable_variants: Ceiling 200 ft (OVC002); CAT I authorized at margin; SFC VIS remark does not override prevailing vis.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §7-1-14: ceiling from lowest BKN/OVC.

prompt: METAR KJFK 170451Z 25036G63KT 1/4SM R04R/P6000FT RA BR SQ BKN004 OVC018 08/07 A2936 RMK AO2 PK WND 22063/0444 SFC VIS 6. What does SQ signify? What does R04R/P6000 mean? Is landing safe?
gold_decision: SQ = squall: sudden wind speed increase ≥16 kt to ≥22 kt sustained ≥1 min — violent convective outflow. PK WND 22063KT at 0444Z is the extreme peak gust. R04R/P6000FT: 'P' prefix = RVR exceeds 6000 ft (upper rollover). Ceiling: BKN004 = 400 ft AGL. Wind 250°/36G63KT on RWY 04R (heading 040°): angle = |250−40| = 210° → supplement: 360−210 = 150°; crosswind = 63 × sin(150°) = 63 × 0.5 = 31.5 kt gust. 63 kt peak total wind is extreme structural wind. Recommendation: HOLD or DIVERT — 63 kt gust exceeds demonstrated crosswind limits for all commercial aircraft; squall indicates rapid condition changes.
required_safety_constraints: SQ: squall — violent rapid wind change; 63 kt gust exceeds all commercial aircraft limits (WMO FM 15).
P6000 RVR = exceeds 6000 ft — do not misread as exactly 6000 ft.
Crosswind = 63 × sin(150°) ≈ 32 kt gust — exceeds most commercial aircraft limits.
Hold/divert until squall passes.
acceptable_variants: Squall with 63 kt gust; crosswind ~32 kt exceeds limits; P6000 RVR = >6000 ft; hold or divert.
evidence_requirements: WMO No. 306 FM 15-XVI: SQ and P-prefix RVR.

prompt: METAR KCVG 040652Z 29011G25KT 1 1/2SM R36C/4500VP6000FT +TSRA BR SCT023CB BKN065 OVC100 17/16 A2978 RMK AO2 PK WND 29034/0639. TAF KCVG shows base FM060800 VRB04KT P6SM BKN080. Current ETA is 0730Z. Can the flight land?
gold_decision: ETA 0730Z. Current METAR at 0652Z shows +TSRA SCT023CB — active heavy thunderstorm with CB at 2300 ft. BKN065 = ceiling 6500 ft. TAF FM060800 (from 0800Z): VRB4KT P6SM BKN080 — VFR. ETA 0730Z is 30 min before TAF clearance at 0800Z. Current +TSRA CB is active 38 min before ETA. Recommendation: hold 20–30 min for TS to clear; reassess at 0715Z. Do not approach active CB. Expect clearing before FM060800 per TAF. Monitor METAR for TSE remark.
required_safety_constraints: Active +TSRA SCT023CB at ETA-38 min: hold — no approach into CB (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
TAF clears at FM060800; ETA 0730Z is 30 min before clearance — hold recommended.
R36C RVR 4500 ft adequate for CAT I once TS clears.
Monitor METAR for TSE remark before commencing approach.
acceptable_variants: Hold 20-30 min for TS clearance; conditions improve by 0800Z per TAF; no approach into active CB.

prompt: METAR KFAR 161953Z 34027G36KT 1 1/2SM R36/4500VP6000FT -RA BR BKN008 OVC016 08/07 A2924 RMK AO2 PK WND 34036/1950 SLP907. OAT +8°C with rain. Is icing a concern? Evaluate approach.
gold_decision: OAT +8°C: no surface airframe icing (requires ≤0°C). Light rain at positive temperature is non-freezing. However, at KFAR in spring, a temperature inversion may place sub-freezing temperatures at altitude — check PIREPs and freezing level forecast for en-route icing. At surface: no icing risk. Ceiling: BKN008 = 800 ft AGL. Visibility: 1.5 SM. CAT I: DH 200 ft < ceiling 800 ft — ceiling clear. RVR 4500 ft > 1800 ft. CAT I authorized. 1-2-3 rule: if TAF shows BKN008/1.5 SM at ETA ±1 hr, ceiling 800 ft < 2000 ft triggers alternate requirement.
required_safety_constraints: OAT +8°C: no surface icing; check freezing level aloft for en-route icing (FAA AC 91-74B).
Ceiling 800 ft > CAT I DH 200 ft; RVR 4500 ft > 1800 ft — CAT I authorized (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
1-2-3 rule: ceiling 800 ft < 2000 ft triggers alternate requirement if TAF shows this at ETA.
34027G36KT on RWY 36 (heading 360°): angle 20°, crosswind = 36 × sin(20°) ≈ 12 kt.
acceptable_variants: No surface icing at +8°C; CAT I authorized; check freezing level aloft; alternate likely required per TAF.

prompt: METAR KBOS 251854Z 26027G36KT 2SM R04R/5500VP6000FT +TSRA BKN060 BKN080CB BKN090 31/23 A2982 RMK AO2 PK WND 25036/1853 RAB48. What is the ceiling: BKN060 or BKN080CB?
gold_decision: Ceiling: BKN060 = 6000 ft AGL (the lowest BKN or OVC layer). BKN080CB is a higher layer at 8000 ft containing CB clouds — significant for turbulence above 8000 ft but not the operational ceiling. The ceiling is always the lowest BKN or OVC layer, regardless of whether higher layers contain CB. Active +TSRA: no approach regardless of ceiling height. Visibility 2 SM < 3 SM — 1-2-3 rule triggered on vis if TAF confirms at ETA.
required_safety_constraints: Ceiling is BKN060 = 6000 ft — not BKN080CB (FAA AIM §7-1-14).
Active +TSRA: no approach regardless of ceiling/vis (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
1-2-3 rule: vis 2 SM < 3 SM — check TAF for alternate requirement.
CB at 8000 ft: severe turbulence above ceiling; do not cruise through CB layer.
acceptable_variants: Ceiling BKN060 = 6000 ft; BKN080CB is higher CB layer; no approach into +TSRA.

prompt: METAR KORD 190151Z 01004KT 1SM R10L/3000VP6000FT +TSRA BR BKN007 BKN017 OVC030CB 22/22 A3000 RMK AO2 SLP154 CONS LTGICCG OHD. What does CONS LTGICCG OHD mean? Is the airport usable?
gold_decision: CONS LTGICCG OHD = continuous lightning in-cloud, cloud-to-cloud, and cloud-to-ground, overhead the airport. OHD means the lightning is directly over the airport. Combined with +TSRA and OVC030CB (CB overcast at 3000 ft), this is a severe thunderstorm directly overhead. Ceiling: BKN007 = 700 ft AGL. Active +TSRA CB overhead: no approach or departure authorized. Lightning strike risk on approach, microburst risk, severe turbulence. Ground operations (fueling, external work) should also be suspended. Recommend hold 20+ nm clear of convection or divert.
required_safety_constraints: CONS LTGICCG OHD: continuous lightning overhead — no approach or departure (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
No approach into +TSRA OVC030CB — CB overhead.
BKN007 = 700 ft ceiling: even if TS cleared, RVR check needed for CAT I.
Ground operations suspended during overhead lightning.
acceptable_variants: Airport not usable — continuous lightning overhead with active +TSRA CB; hold/divert.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §7-1-25: TS and lightning avoidance.

prompt: METAR KFAR 161253Z 35024G37KT 1/4SM R36/3000V5000FT FZFG VV013 M11/M13 A2966 RMK AO2 PK WND 35043/1243 SLP058. B737 ETA 1330Z. TAF shows consistent FZFG OVC003 for 1230-1430Z. Evaluate dispatch.
gold_decision: VV013 = 1300 ft ceiling (sky obscured). RVR R36/3000V5000: lower end 3000 ft > CAT I 1800 ft. Ceiling 1300 ft > CAT I DH 200 ft. TAF for ETA±1 hr (1230–1430Z): FZFG OVC003 — ceiling 300 ft. 1-2-3 rule: TAF ceiling 300 ft < 2000 ft; vis (FZFG, 1/4 SM current) < 3 SM — alternate required. CAT I approach authorized on ceiling and RVR. FZFG: severe airframe icing during approach. PK WND 35043KT on RWY 36 (heading 360°): angle 10°, crosswind = 43 × sin(10°) ≈ 7.5 kt — modest crosswind; headwind 43 kt.
required_safety_constraints: 1-2-3 rule triggered: TAF ceiling OVC003 = 300 ft < 2000 ft; vis < 3 SM — alternate required (14 CFR §121.619).
FZFG: airframe icing on approach — de-ice systems required (14 CFR §121.629).
CAT I authorized on RVR and ceiling.
Crosswind modest (~7.5 kt); headwind 43 kt shortens landing distance.
acceptable_variants: Alternate required per 1-2-3 rule; CAT I authorized; FZFG icing; de-icing needed.

prompt: METAR PAOM 120053Z 21021G30KT 1 1/4SM R28/2800VP6000FT -FZRA BR OVC004 M01/M02 A3019 RMK AO2 PK WND 21031/0037 FZRAB47 SLP224. What does FZRAB47 indicate? What is the icing hazard?
gold_decision: FZRAB47 = freezing rain began at 47 minutes past the hour. Freezing rain (FZRA) produces SLD (supercooled large droplet) icing — accretes behind the leading edge beyond the reach of de-ice boots and TKS systems. At OAT M01°C (-1°C), even slight moisture produces rapid ice accretion. OVC004 = ceiling 400 ft. R28/2800 ft > CAT I 1800 ft. Ceiling 400 ft > CAT I DH 200 ft. CAT I authorized. But: FZRA SLD is a potential in-flight emergency — approach must have all ice protection active. FZRAB47 = onset 13 min ago — active rapid ice accumulation. Runway icing forming rapidly.
required_safety_constraints: FZRA SLD icing: accretes behind leading edge, may exceed aircraft ice protection (FAA AC 91-74B).
FZRAB47: FZRA onset 13 min ago — active rapid ice accumulation.
Runway: -FZRA at M01°C = black ice forming rapidly — RwyCC check essential.
CAT I authorized on ceiling (400 ft) and RVR (2800 ft); ice protection fully active.
acceptable_variants: FZRA SLD onset 13 min ago; severe icing on approach; runway icing; CAT I authorized with ice protection.

prompt: METAR KBOS 011354Z 28017G32KT 1/2SM R04R/2800VP6000FT SN FZFG FEW013 BKN027 OVC065 M01/M03 A2952 RMK AO2 PK WND 29032/1352 SNB41. Ceiling? 1-2-3 rule for ETA 1500Z with TAF showing BKN020 -SN through 1800Z.
gold_decision: Ceiling: BKN027 = 2700 ft AGL (FEW013 is not a ceiling). TAF for ETA±1 hr (1400–1600Z): BKN020 -SN — ceiling 2000 ft exactly. 1-2-3 rule threshold is < 2000 ft (strictly less than); 2000 ft exactly does NOT trigger on ceiling alone. However, -SN likely reduces visibility below 3 SM — if TAF also shows vis < 3 SM at ETA, alternate is required on visibility. Current vis 1/2 SM with -SN confirms visibility is being reduced. FEW013 is not a ceiling; BKN027 is.
required_safety_constraints: 1-2-3 rule: threshold is LESS THAN 2000 ft; TAF BKN020 = exactly 2000 ft — ceiling alone does not trigger (14 CFR §121.619).
TAF visibility with -SN likely < 3 SM — if confirmed, alternate required on vis.
FEW013 is not a ceiling; BKN027 is the ceiling (FAA AIM §7-1-14).
FZFG: de-icing required; runway contamination with SN.
acceptable_variants: Ceiling BKN027 = 2700 ft; 1-2-3 ceiling threshold not triggered at BKN020 exactly; vis check determines alternate need.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §7-1-14: FEW not a ceiling.

prompt: METAR KDFW 250253Z COR 34021G34KT 3/4SM R17C/2400V4500FT +TSRA BR FEW009 OVC017CB 18/17 A2991 RMK AO2 SLP122 FRQ LTGICCG ALQDS. RVR 2400 ft — can a CAT II aircraft attempt approach?
gold_decision: RVR R17C/2400V4500: lower end 2400 ft > CAT II minimum 1200 ft — RVR sufficient for CAT II. However: OVC017CB = overcast CB at 1700 ft. Active +TSRA with FRQ LTGICCG ALQDS (frequent lightning in all quadrants). Active severe thunderstorm prohibits any approach — CAT II certification is irrelevant when TS is active overhead. FRQ lightning in all quadrants: lightning strike risk on approach path. 3/4 SM prevailing vis. Recommendation: NO approach of any category. Hold clear; divert if fuel critical.
required_safety_constraints: Active +TSRA OVC017CB: no approach regardless of RVR or aircraft category (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
FRQ LTGICCG ALQDS: frequent lightning all quadrants — lightning strike risk on approach.
COR flag: use corrected METAR only.
Hold clear; do not attempt CAT II or any approach into active CB/TS.
acceptable_variants: No approach — active TS/CB with lightning all quadrants; RVR adequate but TS prohibits.

prompt: METAR KDEN 290053Z 15024G36KT 5SM R35L/4500VP6000FT +TSRA FEW030 BKN060CB BKN110 BKN200 16/13 A3020 RMK AO2 PK WND 17039/0041. KDEN elevation 5430 ft. Ceiling? Density altitude concern?
gold_decision: Ceiling: BKN060 = 6000 ft AGL (FEW030 is not a ceiling). Active +TSRA BKN060CB. Density altitude at KDEN: OAT 16°C at 5430 ft elevation. Standard temperature at 5430 ft ≈ 4°C. Deviation = +12°C above standard. Density altitude ≈ 5430 + (120 × 12) = ~6870 ft. Reduced engine thrust and longer takeoff/landing rolls. +TSRA BKN060CB: severe convection — no approach into active CB. Mountain thunderstorm with microburst potential.
required_safety_constraints: Active +TSRA BKN060CB: no approach into CB (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
Density altitude ≈6870 ft — increased landing/takeoff distances (FAA-H-8083-25C Ch. 11).
Mountain thunderstorm: rapid development, microburst risk.
BKN060 = ceiling 6000 ft AGL; FEW030 is not ceiling (FAA AIM §7-1-14).
acceptable_variants: No approach — active CB; density altitude ~6870 ft reduces performance; hold until TS clears.

prompt: METAR KMEM 201554Z 16008KT 1/2SM R36L/2000V4500FT +TSRA FG SCT006CB OVC023 18/18 A3009 RMK AO2 TWR VIS 1 SLP189 OCNL LTGICCCCA. RVR lower end 2000 ft — can a non-CAT-II aircraft attempt approach?
gold_decision: RVR R36L lower end 2000 ft > CAT I minimum 1800 ft — sufficient for CAT I on RVR. However: +TSRA FG SCT006CB (CB at 600 ft). Active +TSRA: no approach regardless of RVR. SCT006CB with TS overhead means CB turbulence from 600 ft on final — impossible to stabilize approach. OCNL LTGICCCCA = occasional lightning all types. OVC023 = ceiling 2300 ft (OVC023). TWR VIS 1 SM: controlling vis is lower of surface (1/2 SM) and tower (1 SM) = 1/2 SM. Decision: no approach for any aircraft — active TS/CB.
required_safety_constraints: No approach into +TSRA SCT006CB — CB at 600 ft on final (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
Controlling vis = prevailing 1/2 SM (not tower vis 1 SM) per FAA AC-00-45H.
OCNL LTGICCCCA: lightning — hold until TS clears.
SCT006CB: turbulence risk from 600 ft through approach.
acceptable_variants: No approach — active TS with CB at 600 ft; lightning; hold until TSE.

prompt: METAR KFAR 271253Z 16012G20KT 1/2SM R36/6000VP6000FT FZFG OVC002 M01/M01 A2956 RMK AO2 SLP024 I1001 I6011. What do I1001 and I6011 remarks indicate? Evaluate approach.
gold_decision: I1001 = 0.01 inch ice in past 1 hour; I6011 = 0.11 inch ice in past 6 hours — both are ice accretion remarks confirming FZFG is depositing glaze ice on surfaces. R36/6000VP6000: P6000 = RVR exceeds 6000 ft — not exactly 6000 ft. OVC002 = ceiling 200 ft AGL — CAT I DH exactly. CAT I authorized at absolute margin. Runway icing confirmed (I6011 = 0.11 in over 6 hr) — RwyCC likely ≤1 (nil/poor braking). Must verify RwyCC and runway treatment before approach.
required_safety_constraints: I6011 = 0.11 in ice over 6 hr: runway iced — RwyCC likely ≤1 (FAA AC 150/5200-30D).
OVC002 = 200 ft ceiling: CAT I DH exactly met.
P6000 RVR = exceeds 6000 ft — do not misread as 6000 ft exactly.
Verify runway is treated before approach.
acceptable_variants: Ceiling 200 ft at CAT I limit; P6000 RVR not limiting; runway icing confirmed; RwyCC required.

prompt: METAR KATL 251752Z 12008G25KT 1/4SM -DZ FG OVC002 01/M01 A2981 RMK AO2 SFC VIS 1/2 DZB09FZDZE09 SLP102. What does FZDZE09 indicate for operations?
gold_decision: DZB09FZDZE09: drizzle began at 09 minutes; freezing drizzle ended at 09 minutes. FZDZ occurred briefly and ended at :09, transitioning to plain drizzle at OAT +1°C. Brief FZDZ episode means ice was deposited on cold surfaces before ending. Current -DZ at +1°C is non-freezing, but cold-soaked surfaces may still have ice. OVC002 = ceiling 200 ft — CAT I DH exactly. Visibility 1/4 SM with no RVR reported: prevailing vis 1/4 SM. CAT I approach requires RVR ≥1800 ft when ILS available, or vis ≥½ SM (prevailing) when no RVR. 1/4 SM < ½ SM — approach not authorized without RVR. Request RVR before approach.
required_safety_constraints: FZDZE09: FZDZ ended — but cold surfaces may retain ice (FAA AIM §7-1-20).
OVC002 = 200 ft ceiling = CAT I DH exactly.
1/4 SM prevailing vis without RVR: below CAT I ½ SM prevailing minimum — request RVR (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
Cold soak: verify RwyCC despite FZDZ ending.
acceptable_variants: FZDZ ended but surface ice may remain; request RVR before approach; prevailing vis 1/4 SM below CAT I without RVR.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §5-4-7: CAT I prevailing vis minimum.

prompt: METAR KGTF (Jan 2026 event) 01015G28KT 1SM R21/2600V4000FT -SN FZFG OVC006 M08/M10 A3005 RMK AO2 PK WND 02030/1145 SLP200. KGTF elevation 3677 ft MSL. Ceiling and approach evaluation.
gold_decision: Ceiling: OVC006 = 600 ft AGL. RVR R21/2600V4000: lower end 2600 ft > CAT I 1800 ft. CAT I authorized. Ceiling 600 ft > DH 200 ft. FZFG at M08°C: severe airframe icing. Wind 01015G28KT on RWY 21 (heading 210°): wind 010°, angle = |210−10| = 200° → supplement = 360−200 = 160°; crosswind = 28 × sin(20°) = 9.6 kt; tailwind = 28 × cos(160°) = 28 × (−0.94) ≈ −26 kt → 26 kt tailwind on RWY 21. Use RWY 03 (reciprocal) for headwind advantage.
required_safety_constraints: Tailwind 26 kt on RWY 21 from northerly wind — use RWY 03 (FAA-H-8083-25C Ch. 5).
FZFG at M08°C: severe icing — de-icing mandatory (14 CFR §121.629).
CAT I authorized: OVC006 = 600 ft ceiling; RVR 2600 ft > 1800 ft (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
-SN: RwyCC required before approach.
acceptable_variants: CAT I authorized; use RWY 03 for headwind (tailwind on RWY 21); FZFG severe icing; RwyCC needed.

prompt: METAR KDFW 090353Z 01024G40KT 1 1/4SM +TSRA SCT015 BKN035CB OVC048 22/21 A2981 RMK AO2 PK WND 02040/0353 WSHFT 0329. TAF KDFW PROB30 0904/0907 4SM TSRA BKN025CB. ETA 0500Z. Does PROB30 require an alternate?
gold_decision: ETA 0500Z; window 0400–0600Z. PROB30 0904/0907 = 30% probability TSRA BKN025CB 0400–0700Z on day 9 — overlaps ETA±1 hr window (0400–0600Z). PROB30 conditions: ceiling BKN025CB = 2500 ft > 2000 ft; vis 4 SM > 3 SM — neither PROB30 threshold alone triggers the 1-2-3 rule. The base forecast for ETA must be checked for 1-2-3 compliance. PROB30 alone does not mandate alternate under 14 CFR §121.619. However, current +TSRA CB is active — no approach now. Dispatcher should add alternate for operational prudence given active TS activity near ETA window.
required_safety_constraints: PROB30 does not formally trigger 1-2-3 rule — check base forecast (14 CFR §121.619).
Current +TSRA with CB: no approach; hold until TSE.
PROB30 TSRA CB during ETA window: operationally prudent to add alternate even if not legally required.
Monitor conditions 30 min before ETA for actual TS clearance.
acceptable_variants: PROB30 does not mandate alternate; base forecast determines 1-2-3 rule; prudent to add alternate given active TS.
evidence_requirements: FAA AC 120-77A: use of probabilistic forecasts.

prompt: METAR KMEM 082054Z 33014G27KT 2 1/2SM R36L/2600VP6000FT +TSRA BR SCT012 SCT030CB BKN040 OVC130 24/23 A3008 RMK AO2 PK WND 30030/2024. What is the operational ceiling? Which layer counts?
gold_decision: Cloud layers: SCT012 (scatter, not ceiling), SCT030CB (scatter, not ceiling), BKN040 = ceiling 4000 ft AGL (lowest BKN). OVC130 is higher. SCT030CB at 3000 ft with CB is significant for turbulence even though SCT does not constitute a ceiling. Active +TSRA: no approach regardless of ceiling. Visibility 2.5 SM < 3 SM — 1-2-3 rule check needed. RVR R36L 2600 ft > CAT I 1800 ft. CAT I approach authorized on RVR/ceiling once TS clears.
required_safety_constraints: Ceiling BKN040 = 4000 ft (SCT layers are not ceilings) (FAA AIM §7-1-14).
SCT030CB: CB at 3000 ft — turbulence from 3000 ft regardless of ceiling status.
Active +TSRA: no approach (FAA AIM §7-1-25).
1-2-3 rule: vis 2.5 SM < 3 SM — check TAF for alternate.
acceptable_variants: Ceiling BKN040 = 4000 ft; SCT030CB not ceiling but CB turbulence risk; no approach into +TSRA.

prompt: METAR KDEN 281053Z 02018G26KT 1/2SM R35L/3500VP6000FT SN FZFG FEW008 BKN013 OVC045 M08/M10 A3000 RMK AO2 PK WND 01026/1048 TWR VIS 3/4. Controlling visibility? Ceiling? Evaluate.
gold_decision: Controlling visibility: 1/2 SM (prevailing, lower) vs TWR VIS 3/4 SM — controlling = 1/2 SM per FAA AC-00-45H. Ceiling: BKN013 = 1300 ft AGL (lowest BKN; FEW008 is not a ceiling). OVC045 is higher. RVR R35L/3500 ft > CAT I 1800 ft. Ceiling 1300 ft > CAT I DH 200 ft. CAT I authorized on ceiling and RVR. For ILS, RVR governs over prevailing vis. FZFG at M08°C: severe icing — de-icing required. SN: runway contaminated — RwyCC needed.
required_safety_constraints: Controlling vis = 1/2 SM (lower of surface and tower per FAA AC-00-45H).
Ceiling BKN013 = 1300 ft; FEW008 is not a ceiling (FAA AIM §7-1-14).
CAT I authorized: ceiling 1300 ft, RVR 3500 ft.
FZFG M08°C: severe icing; KDEN SN runway contamination.
acceptable_variants: Controlling vis 1/2 SM; ceiling BKN013 = 1300 ft; CAT I authorized; FZFG extreme icing at KDEN.
